description: Allowlist for test_arch_goal_gate.py Gate A. An entry exempts (path-prefix, pattern) findings that are legitimately platform-specific — device construction / detection / backend registration at the composition root, where branching on the accelerator is correct by design (analogous to backend_boundary_allowlist.json). The LLM hot-path coupling (env reads, OnceLock config freeze, supports_*() behavior branches) is NOT allowlisted: those are real decoupling targets tracked in STATUS.md and must reach zero by migration, not exemption.

entries.reason: Backend/device registration is the composition root: it must branch on cuda/metal availability to register the right backend and enumerate devices. This is device wiring, not LLM forward-path behavior.
entries.review_condition: Re-review if device/backend registration moves out of registry.rs or stops needing compile-time backend gating.

entries.reason: Maps the ferrum Device enum to a concrete candle Device (creating CUDA/Metal devices). Device construction is inherently platform-specific and backend-agnostic model code never sees it.
entries.review_condition: Re-review if tensor-factory device construction is unified behind a runtime resolver.

entries.reason: Device detection/enumeration (detect_metal_devices, per-device capability probing). Platform-specific by nature; sits below the model abstraction.
entries.review_condition: Re-review if device detection is delegated to the kernels backend crate.
